# KAI — Enterprise AI Development Platform (Full Documentation)

> Four Claude Code plugins for AI-assisted enterprise development on Azure DevOps and Atlassian. 70+ skills that run identically across Claude Code, GitHub Copilot CLI, and VS Code Chat — from ticket to PR, fully autonomous. Deep AEM specialization built in.

## What This Is

KAI is a structured development workflow built as a plugin system for enterprise teams. It encodes the full sprint lifecycle — requirements analysis, implementation planning, code generation, testing, verification, documentation, and PR creation — into **skills** that orchestrate multi-agent pipelines across every major AI platform. A single command like `/dx-req` pulls the ticket from Azure DevOps or Jira, validates readiness against your DoR, distills developer requirements, researches the codebase with parallel subagents, and generates a team summary.

## Architecture

### Four-Plugin Design

Plugins are independently installable. Non-AEM projects only need dx-core (+ dx-hub for multi-repo).

**dx-core** (45 skills, 6 agents)
Platform-agnostic ADO/Jira workflow: requirements → planning → execution → review → PR. Works with any tech stack. Includes 15 Copilot agent templates (including coordinators).

**dx-aem** (12 skills, 6 agents)
AEM-specific verification, QA, and demo capture. Includes AEM project knowledge (seed data). Requires dx-core.

**dx-hub** (3 skills)
Multi-repo orchestration: hub init, config, status.

**dx-automation** (11 skills)
Autonomous AI agents (DoR checker, DoD checker, DoD fixer, PR reviewer, PR answerer, BugFix agent, QA agent, DevAgent, DOCAgent, Estimation) running 24/7 as ADO pipelines triggered by AWS Lambda webhooks. Requires dx-core.

### Plugin Structure

Each plugin follows this layout:
```
plugin/
├── .claude-plugin/plugin.json   # Plugin manifest
├── .mcp.json                    # MCP server config
├── agents/                      # Agent definitions (YAML frontmatter + markdown)
├── skills/                      # Skill directories (*/SKILL.md)
├── rules/                       # Default prompt templates
├── hooks/                       # Plugin hooks
├── data/                        # Seed files copied to project by init
├── shared/                      # Reference files read by skills
└── templates/                   # Init-time file templates
```

### Config-Driven Design

All project-specific values live in `.ai/config.yaml` (generated by `/dx-init`, extended by `/aem-init`). Skills read config at runtime. Instead of hardcoding `mvn clean install`, read `build.command`. Instead of `develop`, read `scm.base-branch`. Instead of `http://localhost:4502`, read `aem.url`.

### Spec Directory Convention

Per-ticket output goes to `.ai/specs/<id>-<slug>/` with predictable filenames (`raw-story.md`, `explain.md`, `research.md`, `implement.md`, etc.). Skills find each other's output by convention — no data passing needed.

### Model Tier Strategy

| Tier | Use | Agents / Skills |
|------|-----|-----------------|
| Opus | Deep reasoning (code review, planning, verification) | dx-code-reviewer agent; dx-plan, dx-step-verify, dx-pr-review skills |
| Sonnet | Execution (steps, PR review, inspections) | dx-pr-reviewer agent, aem-inspector; dx-step, dx-req, dx-step-fix skills |
| Haiku | Simple lookups (file search, doc search) | dx-file-resolver, dx-doc-searcher agents; dx-ticket-analyze, dx-help skills |

### MCP Server Integrations

- **Azure DevOps**: Work items, PRs, repos, code search
- **Atlassian**: Jira issues, Confluence pages
- **Figma**: Design extraction, screenshots, tokens
- **axe**: Accessibility testing
- **AEM**: JCR content, components, dialogs (HTTP MCP)
- **Chrome DevTools**: Browser automation, screenshots

## Skill Catalog

### dx-core — 45 Skills

**Estimation (1 skill)**
- `/dx-estimate <work-item-id(s)>` — Analyze story and produce structured estimation (hours/SP, plan, open questions). Posts as ADO/Jira comment.

**Requirements + DoR (5 skills)**
- `/dx-dor <work-item-id(s)>` — Validate Definition of Ready against wiki checklist.
- `/dx-req <work-item-id>` — Full requirements pipeline: fetch ticket, validate DoR, distill requirements, research codebase, share summary.
- `/dx-req-tasks <work-item-id>` — Create child Task work items with hour estimates.
- `/dx-req-dod <work-item-id>` — Check Definition of Done and auto-fix gaps.
- `/dx-req-import <path>` — Validate external requirements document.

**Figma (4 skills)**
- `/dx-figma-all [ADO ID] [Figma URL]` — Full Figma workflow: extract → prototype → verify.
- `/dx-figma-extract [ADO ID] [Figma URL]` — Extract design context, tokens, screenshots from Figma.
- `/dx-figma-prototype <work-item-id>` — Research conventions + generate high-fidelity HTML/CSS prototype.
- `/dx-figma-verify <work-item-id>` — Visually verify prototype against Figma reference.

**Planning (3 skills)**
- `/dx-plan <work-item-id>` — Generate step-by-step implementation plan with status tracking.
- `/dx-plan-validate <work-item-id>` — Verify plan covers all requirements.
- `/dx-plan-resolve <work-item-id>` — Research and fix risks flagged by validation.

**Execution (5 skills)**
- `/dx-step-all <work-item-id>` — Execute all plan steps autonomously (step → fix loop).
- `/dx-step <work-item-id>` — Execute next pending step (implement, test, review, commit).
- `/dx-step-fix <work-item-id>` — Diagnose and fix a blocked step.
- `/dx-step-build` — Build and deploy using config build command, auto-fix errors.
- `/dx-step-verify <work-item-id>` — 6-phase verification: compile, lint, test, secret scan, architecture, code review.

**Pull Request (7 skills)**
- `/dx-pr <work-item-id>` — Create PR via ADO MCP with generated description.
- `/dx-pr-commit [pr]` — Commit changes with ADO work item linking.
- `/dx-pr-review <PR-id or URL>` — Review a single PR with findings and fix patches.
- `/dx-pr-review-all` — Batch-review multiple open PRs.
- `/dx-pr-answer <PR-id or URL>` — Answer open PR comments with codebase context.
- `/dx-pr-review-report <PR-id or URL>` — Generate categorized report from PR review.
- `/dx-pr-reviews-report [--any] [PR URL | Repo URL | count]` — Batch-generate review reports.

**Bug Fix (4 skills)**
- `/dx-bug-all <bug-id>` — Full bug workflow: triage → verify → fix.
- `/dx-bug-triage <bug-id>` — Fetch bug, find affected component, save triage.
- `/dx-bug-verify <bug-id> [before|after|qa]` — Reproduce bug via Chrome DevTools.
- `/dx-bug-fix <bug-id>` — Generate fix plan from triage, execute, create PR.

**Agent Roles (3 skills)**
- `/dx-agent-all <work-item-id>` — Full pipeline: RE → plan → develop → review → PR.
- `/dx-agent-re <work-item-id>` — RE Agent: analyze story, produce structured requirements spec.
- `/dx-agent-dev <work-item-id>` — Dev Agent: implement from RE spec, self-check, commit.

**Documentation (2 skills)**
- `/dx-doc <work-item-id>` — Generate technical documentation from implementation.
- `/dx-help [topic]` — Search project documentation and conventions.

**Utility skills**
- `/dx-init` — Initialize project config and seed data.
- `/dx-ticket-analyze <work-item-id>` — Quick ticket analysis with file resolution.
- `/dx-config` — View/edit project configuration.
- `/dx-status` — Show project and workflow status.

### dx-aem — 12 Skills

- `/aem-init` — Initialize AEM-specific config and seed data.
- `/aem-inspect <component>` — Deep component inspection: dialog, model, HTL, JCR.
- `/aem-editorial-qa <page-url>` — Editorial QA with browser automation.
- `/aem-editorial-guide-capture` — Capture editorial guidelines from live pages.
- `/aem-demo-capture <page-url>` — Screenshot-based demo capture.
- `/aem-bug-executor <bug-id>` — AEM-specific bug fix execution.
- Plus 6 additional AEM verification and content skills.

### dx-hub — 3 Skills

- `/dx-hub-init` — Initialize multi-repo hub configuration.
- `/dx-hub-config` — View/edit hub-level configuration.
- `/dx-hub-status` — Cross-repo status overview.

### dx-automation — 11 Skills

- `/auto-dor` — Autonomous DoR validation (pipeline trigger).
- `/auto-dod` — Autonomous DoD checking.
- `/auto-dod-fix` — Autonomous DoD auto-fix.
- `/auto-pr-review` — Autonomous PR review.
- `/auto-pr-answer` — Autonomous PR comment response.
- `/auto-bugfix` — Autonomous bug fix.
- `/auto-qa` — Autonomous QA verification.
- `/auto-dev` — Autonomous development agent.
- `/auto-doc` — Autonomous documentation generation.
- `/auto-estimate` — Autonomous estimation.
- `/auto-setup` — Configure automation infrastructure.

## Agent Catalog

### dx-core — 6 Agents

| Agent | Model | Role |
|-------|-------|------|
| dx-code-reviewer | Opus | Deep code review with 80%+ confidence filtering |
| dx-pr-reviewer | Sonnet | PR diff analysis with structured findings |
| dx-file-resolver | Haiku | Resolve source files for components/modules |
| dx-doc-searcher | Haiku | Search project docs and reference files |
| dx-figma-styles | Haiku | Discover CSS/SCSS conventions from project |
| dx-figma-markup | Haiku | Discover HTML/markup conventions from project |

### dx-aem — 6 Agents

| Agent | Model | Role |
|-------|-------|------|
| aem-inspector | Sonnet | Deep AEM component inspection |
| aem-page-finder | Haiku | Find AEM pages by component usage |
| aem-dialog-analyzer | Sonnet | Analyze AEM dialog definitions |
| aem-content-reader | Haiku | Read JCR content nodes |
| aem-editorial-capture | Sonnet | Capture editorial guidelines from live pages |
| aem-bug-executor | Sonnet | AEM-specific bug fix execution |

### Copilot CLI Agents — 25 Templates

Copilot agent templates mirror Claude Code agents for VS Code Chat and Copilot CLI compatibility. Includes coordinator agents that orchestrate multi-step workflows.

## Workflows

### Sprint Lifecycle (Ticket to PR)

1. `/dx-req <ticket>` — Requirements analysis (fetch, validate DoR, distill, research, share)
2. `/dx-figma-all <figma-url>` — Design-to-code (extract, prototype, verify) [optional]
3. `/dx-plan` — Implementation planning with dependency tracking
4. `/dx-step-all` — Autonomous execution (step → test → fix loop)
5. `/dx-step-verify` — 6-phase verification gate
6. `/dx-pr` — Create PR with generated description

### Bug Fix Workflow

1. `/dx-bug-triage <bug-id>` — Fetch and analyze bug
2. `/dx-bug-verify <bug-id> before` — Reproduce with browser automation
3. `/dx-bug-fix` — Generate fix plan and execute
4. `/dx-bug-verify <bug-id> after` — Verify fix locally

### Figma-to-Code Pipeline

1. `/dx-figma-extract` — Extract design context from Figma via MCP
2. `/dx-figma-prototype` — Generate convention-aware HTML/CSS prototype
3. `/dx-figma-verify` — Visual comparison and auto-fix (max 2 rounds)
4. `/dx-plan` + `/dx-step-all` — Production implementation from verified prototype

### Autonomous Pipeline (24/7)

Triggered by AWS Lambda webhooks on ADO events:
- Ticket tagged → DoR validation → requirements analysis
- PR created → automated review → findings posted
- Bug assigned → triage → fix → PR creation
- All using the same skills that run locally

## Installation

```bash
# Add local marketplace
/plugin marketplace add /path/to/dx-aem-flow

# Install plugins
/plugin install dx-core@dx-aem-flow
/plugin install dx-aem@dx-aem-flow   # optional, for AEM projects

# Initialize
/dx-init
/aem-init    # optional, for AEM projects
```

### Standalone CLI (No Claude Code Required)

```bash
node cli/bin/dx-scaffold.js /tmp/test-project --all
```
